mechanism_narrative: Housing starts lead economic activity by 6 to 9 months; a level above 1,500,000 units signals robust construction demand that translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is above 1500k, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the signal's rolling 1-year Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% indicates a softening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 5.0% within a 3-month rolling window
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, the average forward 60-day SPX return is greater than +2%, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation rises while the federal funds rate remains accommodative, real interest rates become negative, eroding purchasing power and signaling future monetary tightening; this depresses equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when both CPIAUCSL is above 260 and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, the forward 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 60% of cases, or the factor-mimicking portfolio has a Sharpe ratio below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A two‑month consecutive decline in industrial production signals an emerging contraction in manufacturing output; during the mid-to-late business cycle when inventories are elevated, this contraction amplifies the negative impact on corporate profits and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle with elevated inventory-to-sales ratio
failure_condition: After two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO, the forward 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is greater than 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls above 150,000 indicate a healthy labor market that supports consumption and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market absorbs the employment data.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS is above 150,000, the forward 5-day SPX return is negative in more than 45% of instances, or the annualized information ratio is below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in retail and food services sales exceeding 1.0% signals strong consumer spending that boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market quickly prices in the demand strength.
failure_condition: After RSAFS growth exceeds 1.0%, the forward 5-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is less than 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with accelerating nonfarm payrolls growth creates a synergistic positive impulse for the economy: construction employment boosts spending and new homes drive durable goods demand, producing higher S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both HOUST is above 1500k and PAYEMS is above 150,000, the forward 60-day SPX return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the joint signal's Rank IC is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate of at least 25 basis points over six months signals an accommodative monetary pivot that lowers discount rates and stimulates economic activity; during the early stages of an easing cycle following a tightening peak, this effect is strongest for positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 25bp over a 6-month period
regime_hint: easing cycle following a tightening peak
failure_condition: After a 25bp+ decline in FEDFUNDS over 6 months, the forward 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases, or the cumulative return over the next 20 days averages less than 0.5%.
